[02:03]

[02:13]
"> Zero."
[02:15]

[02:42]
"For him, I mean, he's in his mid20s. That could compound for like two decades before he needs to touch it and turn into some serious fucking cash. But he hadn't invested a dollar."
[02:54]

[03:20]

[03:36]
"So why does one guy invest 42k a month making far less and why does the other guy who's making triple not invest a dollar? What do you think the difference there is mentally? What do you think the perspective between those two individuals is?"
[03:52]
"> Either like not understanding how it is or lack of discipline."
[03:57]

[04:15]
"Like 42K a month to be clear over the investment horizon of doing that monthly every fucking month for like 15 20 years. You know how much money that turns into?"
[04:27]

[04:45]

[05:03]

[05:14]

[05:25]

[05:40]
"And I I think I cracked it. Okay, I reflected on my own life and and where I've been and what initially led me to getting to the point where I would invest that much."
[05:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Four Financial Levels"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado numericamente no momento
VISUAL: Estrutura hierárquica de níveis financeiros: Survival → Comfort → Abundance → Excess
[05:55]
"The first step of life is typically survival. After you get through survival, survival sucks. Okay, you've all experienced survival. Survival is a mental place where you don't feel secure in whatever you're making currently."
[06:10]
"Survival is a place of a lot of times it's poverty. But you can still experience survival when you're making tens of thousands of dollars a month and your expenditures are high and you don't feel like you have enough to be able to maintain your bills."
[06:25]
"Survival is a mental state of like a very shortterm perspective. Survival is like a daybyday, minuteby minute, like hourby-hour view on your life. you know, meal by meal for some people when it gets that short term."
[06:40]
"Okay, a lot of you obviously are not even close to fucking survival. The next level above that is comfort."
[06:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Comfort Level Characteristics"
NÚMEROS: $10K-$100K/month, $10K-$30K credit card bills, <$5K-$8K rent
VISUAL: Indicadores de lifestyle comfort: primeiras 10 filas em jogos, restaurantes desejados, primeiro carro premium (AMG/M series), perspectiva mês-a-mês
[06:50]
"Comfort becomes, okay, you're probably making anywhere from like 10 to maybe even 100k a month and you've you've started to spend money, okay, you go to the restaurants that you've always wanted to go to that you see other people flexing."
[07:05]
"When you go to basketball games, you don't get to sit courtside, okay? You you sit in one of the first like 10 rows, you know, and you get to go whenever you really want. You don't really think about it."
[07:18]
"Your credit card bills for the first time, you're kind of impressed, you know? You're spending like 10 to 30k a month and you're like, 'Wow, you know, I didn't think I could spend that amount of money and be able to pay it off every month.' Wow."
[07:32]
"your rent probably something super fucking small that you'll be really really appreciative of one day that you kept it that low but probably somewhere like less than 5k a month you know at most maybe 8k a month is what you're paying to live"
[07:45]
"you might have like one car okay maybe a AMG or a fucking M series or some shit like that you're not doing much comfort is a level that you know feels good no longer survival but it's still a very short-term view"
[07:58]
"it's generally like a month overmonth view on life, okay? You're no longer technically paycheck to paycheck. You're month by month. Okay? Does that make sense so far?"
[08:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Abundance Level Reality"
NÚMEROS: $100K-$200K/month spending, $1,000 dinners (2 pessoas), primeira fila (não courtside), primeira classe (não private)
VISUAL: Lifestyle de abundância: gastos sem pensar, roupas sem limite de preço, staff sob demanda, contas AmEx altas pagas facilmente, fluxo positivo constante
[08:12]
"The level above that, which is where honestly I previously thought things were capped. I didn't actually anticipate that there was a greater level than this. And this is where I think I made my mistake, which is after comfort comes abundance."
[08:28]
"Okay? And I think, tell me if I'm wrong, most people I think that's the level that you want to achieve. Do you have a ceiling that comes above abundance in your head right now?"
[08:40]
"Really think, is there a level above abundance in your view on where you can get to financially? Think about what that means to you."
[08:50]
"What it meant for me when I first felt like I experienced abundance was, you know, I'd go to some restaurant with like two people, like me and me and a girl, and I'd spend like $1,000 on dinner. I wouldn't give a fuck"
[09:03]
"I'd get like whatever on the menu looked good, eat like a bite of it, you know? You want the leftovers, sir? Fucking no. I don't eat leftovers. You know what I mean?"
[09:14]
"You're officially going like when I go to the Heat games, it's like I'm sitting in the first row, but I'm not courtside yet. You know, it's like you go flying first class everywhere you go. You'd never fucking fly economy a day in your life again. But you're not flying private in abundance yet."
[09:30]
"Those kind of bills still hurt, you know? But you got enough excess where clothes don't matter to you anymore in terms of cost of things."
[09:40]
"like the the regular stuff that used to be something you have to give a fuck about, you don't care about at all and you do it in such a high frequency and you still don't feel the effects of it."
[09:52]
"Your MX bills, like when I felt like I was in abundance initially, I was probably spending like 100 to 200k a month and just ripping those MX bills, you know, always paying everything off. Didn't really matter much."
[10:05]
"You know, the amount of money coming into my bank account was always far greater than the amount leaving my bank account. Abundance was like, you know, if I needed a new staff member, it wasn't really even a thought. It was just, yeah, let's do it. add another payroll position."
[10:20]
"Abundance is like, you know, I pay my chiropractor 5K a month to come over here four times in the fucking month. You know what I mean? It's like people are coming to you now. Your doctors, your barbers, like all that kind of shit starts happening in abundance. Okay?"
[10:35]
"And more importantly than anything else, in a perspective place, and this is the most important part, from a perspective on where you think you're at financially, you officially mark yourself as like, I'm doing good."
[10:48]
"Okay? you feel like complete. You know, you're doing a lot of things in in literally abundance. Nothing really matters anymore from a financial perspective."
[10:58]
"But I want to be clear once again, you're still not doing the big things. You're still not putting up 200k a month in investments. You're still not fucking stuff like I pay 25K a month in life insurance on four different policies."
[11:12]
"You know, I don't give a fuck I don't even recognize when those things withdraw from my account. You know, I at one point had 12 cars. I don't even know what I spend on cars right now. I have no clue."
[11:25]
"You could ask me how much my insurance is. I have no idea. You know, no clue. Abundance. That's what happens. You know what I mean? You don't You don't really recognize that kind of shit anymore. Do you get what I'm saying? Okay."
[11:38]
"I view 19K a month for this place as cheap. It's a fucking deal. You know, that's the kind of mindset abundance put you in."
[11:48]
"You You get what I'm saying? I think the guy making 300K a month was experiencing abundance, but you there was an issue there. He still wasn't investing in the long term."
[12:00]
"So ask yourself this, was he not making enough to make the investments consistently or was there a perspective gap? Is there something greater than the abundance that we have to look at and shift that view into in order for him to do the long-term things?"
[12:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "EXCESS - The Game Changer"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado numericamente
VISUAL: Conceito de "excesso" como nível superior à abundância, breakthrough mental para investimentos massivos
[12:20]
"And this is why I unlocked a new level. This is when I recognized in myself when I initially crossed this barrier from abundance. This was the next level, which is the most important level for everybody here to understand and to try to work your way into, which is excess."
[12:38]
"Okay, pull out your phone right now. Google the definition of excess. Take 30 seconds. Go on Google. Type in excess define. Read what it says. Somebody read it out loud."
[12:50]
"> An amount of something that is more than necessary, permitted, or desirable."
[12:55]
"That right there. When you have so much fucking money, you genuinely don't have any clue what the fucking else you could spend it on. Okay."
[13:05]
"Does that not sound like the perfect word to define when you'd be stuffing 42k a month into investments, 200k a month into investments? You know, does that not sound like the perfect barrier to go above that abundance view?"
[13:20]
"So, when I looked at this, I then tried to analyze those two people again. I tried to say to myself, all right, how do you shift from abundance to excess? Okay."
[13:32]
"And the mental barrier that you cross is one of the most important mental barriers that you have to work through in order to truly get wealthy."
[13:42]
"Almost all of you here right now, you live like a fucking rich dude in the modern day and age. Okay? You got enough cash, like I said, to fly here, however the fuck you flew here to like have excess hobby."
[13:55]
"Like when I got into different hobbies, you know, I bought like fucking 400 grand in watches in a couple months, you know? I bought like tens of thousand dollars in guns in a couple months."
[14:08]
"Like I don't even use those things anymore. You know, I wear this watch every fucking day. I haven't shot a gun in two years. You know, these kind of things."
[14:18]
"I want to be clear once again. Why? And how do we cross into outside of just the money we're making? Okay, because once again, one guy was making less money than the other. One guy was making more money than the other by triple the amount."
[14:33]
"What is the difference mentally that occurred to push one individual into excess to make those kinds of investments? Okay."

VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado de dois membros, mesma educação, resultados opostos de investimento

[15:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Three Transcenders"
NÚMEROS: 3 fatores principais
VISUAL: Framework triangular: Income (receita) + People (pessoas ao redor) + Circumstances (situações/coisas materiais)
[15:48]
"There's a very critical thing I need you to understand real quick. Okay? There's three things. Remember how I always talk about identity? I tell you guys, I'm like, 'Your identity and the characteristics of self, they're fluid.'"
[16:03]
"We get to choose like a Grand Theft Auto character, you know, we get to make ourselves up for like what we are and how we view ourselves. And that character ends up controlling a lot about what we experience in life and what we do and like the actions that we take."
[16:20]
"These different levels of survival, comfort, abundance, and excess all have very specific mental frames that pair with them that evolve as you shift your identity and grow into new versions of yourself as a character. Okay, follow along with me here."
[16:38]
"To get to the point where you cross from excess, from abundance into excess, we have to define what abundance is to you. Every version of these levels here have a specific set of characteristics."
[16:52]
"Self-aware. Be self-aware with me real quick. When I talked about survival, what did you think in your head that meant when I told you things like, you know, there are poor people at that level?"
[17:05]
"There's people who make tens of thousands of dollars a month who experience survival. Do you notice how you had a whole set of like conditions that occurred when I mentioned it starts with survival? Did you recognize that in yourself? Think about this."
[17:20]

[17:38]
"Think about the person you were when you transcended from survival to comfort. Okay, what were the differences that occurred? Remember when you were poor? Like what was the difference? Think about once again the traits of yourself. Think about the beliefs that had to shift."
[17:55]

[18:05]
"If you didn't add specific characteristics and worldviews to make you value this kind of thing, you likely didn't care about this kind of stuff at all when you were broke. You wouldn't understand it. Think about this."
[18:18]
"You had a character that when you hit a certain level, did you consciously shift from survival to comfort? Like did you just get a badge that said like, 'Hey man, congratulations. You know, you're at comfort now.' Did that happen?"
[18:32]
"How did you get to this point? It wasn't conscious at all, right? It wasn't an effort where you really thought and made like an effortbased transition."
[18:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Three Transcenders Explained"
NÚMEROS: 3 fatores que movem entre níveis
VISUAL: Breakdown detalhado: 1) INCOME (receita/banco), 2) PEOPLE (relacionamentos/círculo), 3) CIRCUMSTANCES (posses/situações)
[18:45]
"There's three things that transcend these different levels, move you up and down between them. Number one, income."
[18:53]
"I had a client was doing $2.5 million a month for several months. Okay. He hit a point where he made a certain amount of money that like a new character unlocked for him."
[19:05]
"It was like, you ever seen somebody that starts acting erratic and it ends up they had like a tumor or something in their head that made them erratic after they do a brain scan? It's like they didn't know that they all of a sudden changed their behavior. They just had a fucking tumor in their head that changed everything for them."
[19:23]
"Think about this. When you make a certain amount of money, like this guy who was doing $2 half million dollars a month, his character completely pivoted to now I need to do private equity. Now I need to go invest."
[19:38]
"He invested in 40 different companies. He split his attention from his main income 41 different ways. Okay. He went from 12 staff to 200 plus staff."
[19:50]
"He started using terms like did you know offices are graded? like there's A-rated offices, B-rated offices, C-rated offices in terms like the class of office that there is. He started talking about he started giving a fuck about things like Aclass offices."
[20:08]
"We were all fully remote when he got to $2.5 million a month. What made that happen? Why did all that behavior all of a sudden just come out?"
[20:18]
"Number one, income. He reached a certain amount financially, whether in his bank account or just month over month, he maintained a certain income for a sustained period of time."
[20:30]
"Most likely the accumulation of cash to be clear. He probably saw a certain amount in his bank account and said, 'Oh, I reached a point where this particular next character needs to come out now that I've hit this specific milestone.'"
[20:45]
"Remember what I said? Do you transcend and descend these levels consciously? Do you make a choice to go from comfort to abundance or does it just happen? Most of the time it just happens."
[20:58]
"And once again, trait number one that makes this occur is your income. You might just make a certain amount of money and all of a sudden everything about you changes. Your identity changes. You just evolve into a specific thing. Does that make sense?"

[26:10]

[28:03]

[28:18]
"When I go to a restaurant, it's like I got invited to this place the other night cuz I'm a top 10 highest spending customer at this fucking place where you drop easily a,000 to $2,000 every meal. I go like twice a month."
[28:33]
"Okay. Little money. It's pretty much free, like Ben would say. Point is, this place, I love it. It's amazing. I don't give a fuck what it costs. It's more about the quality of what I'm getting and the food and the experience. Okay? To me, that's excess."
[28:50]
"I'm I'm attempting to articulate to you something very important. It's a personal set of those three characteristics of the people, the income, and the circumstances, things, and stuff that shift you between these these different levels: survival, comfort, abundance, and excess."
[29:08]
"Once again, one guy doing 300K a month is still not in excess and not investing a fucking dollar. One guy making 100K a month net is $42,000 a month investing. Why? What the fuck else can he spend money on? Am I wrong?"
[29:25]
"That's what made you spend $42,000 a month. Correct? You said to yourself, I guarantee it internally. You said, 'I don't know what the fuck else I could buy.'"
[29:35]
"> Yeah, that's pretty much it."

"You know, go buy a duck that's like $300 or some fucking You know what I mean? Like something that just you feel in your chest, you're like, you know, that's it right there."
[57:13]
"And then do go buy like a fucking $300 shirt. You know what I mean? I'm sure that'll give you a little sensation and then eventually that'll go away and it'll be normal to you"
[57:25]
"and then you'll know that you have successfully pulled yourself up just a little bit further until eventually you cross the threshold into the next level. Does that make sense?"
[57:35]
"> Yeah."
[57:38]
"And remember, you also can do fucking that fucking pushes you down. Okay? And like I said, you got to identify what those behaviors are. You got to find the things right now that you're actively doing that are fucking you and keeping you down on the lower levels of the spectrum. Does that make sense?"
[57:55]
"> Yeah."
[57:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Leo's Car Upgrade Story"
NÚMEROS: $30K/mês de renda, Corolla → Mercedes SUV (presente), 4 filhos + esposa
VISUAL: Exemplo de responsabilidade por outros: coach identificando âncoras nos membros do time, intervenção para elevar nível
[58:00]
"A lot of times it's like, dude, I'll give you a perfect example. And I take responsibility for others around me. Leo, what kind of car did you have? You're making like $30,000 a month. What kind of cars were you driving?"
[58:15]
"> fucking Corolla."
[58:18]
"You know how you know how pissed I was to hear that my top fucking sales guy that's been working with me for like a decade was driving a Corolla? What did I do? I go I just gave him a fucking Mercedes SUV, you know? I'm like, 'Here, you need this.' Okay."
[58:35]
"And no joke, it made him more money. It made me more money. It made him more money because he doesn't recognize initially that like something like a you have four kids, you know? You have a fucking wife. It's like how do they even fit in a Corolla?"
[58:52]
"You know what I mean? It's like you leaving kids at home. It's so it's like you level up. It's like, you see what I mean? It's like and it changes everything. You get what I'm saying?"
[59:05]
"So, so the moral of the story is like I take responsibility not only for myself. It's like I take responsibility to identify others around me that are fucking"
[59:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Environment Control Examples"
NÚMEROS: Masseuse $1K+/mês apartamento com mofo = fired, Marina $5K para sair do emprego (fazia $3-4K/mês)
VISUAL: Proteção de ambiente: eliminar pessoas/situações que puxam para baixo, pagar para resolver problemas de proximidade

"Look, I had a masseuse come over here the other day. I don't even remember her name. I don't want to remember her name. She while she was giving me a sauce, she talked the whole fucking time about mold and like how mold and this like shitty thousand something a month apartment she was living in like fucking her whole life up."

[01:00:28]

[01:00:45]

[01:01:20]

[01:03:32]

[01:03:48]

[01:04:03]

[01:05:05]
"> Yeah."
[01:05:08]
"And I'm sometimes I feel as though like I'm over obsessing about invest right now, but I'm not."
[01:05:15]
"You just start. It's like you'd ra you should start because the process of starting is better than not doing anything."
[01:05:25]
"And to be clear, it's like, okay, just remember what I said, which is like I want way nicer cars than what I got now. Okay? But at the same time, it's like I got the cars that I got now. I'm still happy I have those. You know, I'm still fulfilled. I got those. I'm still having fun."
[01:05:43]
"But it's like it's a step. You don't just go, remember what I said? You don't just go from here to fucking here. You got to level up in between. And those incremental levels, they typically come from time and action. They don't come from thinking about things. They come from time and action and consistency in those variables."
[01:06:03]
"So, long story short, it's like, dude, if you invest like $100, okay? Start by doing something small. Every time you go out to eat, invest the same amount as whatever the bill is, some long-term fucking okay?"
[01:06:20]
"And feel small by doing it, but at least you did it because that in itself will reinforce to you like, man, I'm not fucking doing enough yet. And then that will perpetuate you wanting to do more, which in itself will get you more. Does that make sense?"
[01:06:38]
"Versus if you do nothing, you progressed zero."
[01:06:42]
"I've been investing like 2 to 10k a month."
[01:06:48]
"Great."
[01:06:50]
"Like so I'm already doing I've already been doing it."
[01:06:53]
"Yeah. Great."
[01:06:55]
"But I I find myself like over obsessing about the amount that I am investing. I'm not even making enough money to really like"
[01:07:05]
"Do you feel what I'm talking about with the cycle of okay, as you do it and you feel it's not enough and too small. Okay. Okay. And you're like obsessing about the fact it should be more. That that in itself perpetuates you wanting to get richer and there in turn you get richer because of that. Can you recognize that?"
[01:07:28]
"> Yeah. I just at some sometimes I feel like it's holding me back how much I'm like obsessing over because I'm like soated on it that like for example like I'm I'm hiring a marketing person to help me with certain fucking Like I was so like focused on whether or not I want to do that because of the fact that I would be less profitable to be able to invest. You know what I'm saying?"
[01:07:53]
"But what's the conclusion of that? The conclusion is what? I need to"
[01:07:58]
"> make more money."
[01:08:00]
"Yeah, that's right. So that thought in itself does what to you?"
[01:08:05]
"> Makes me need to make more money."
[01:08:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Positive Obsession Cycle"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Loop virtuoso: obsessão com investimento → percepção de insuficiência → necessidade de ganhar mais → ação para ganhar mais → mais investimento
[01:08:10]
"That's fucking right. Like Nick, in excess. Okay. It's like the only fucking reason I continue to want to get richer is because there's so much more fucking you know, that I genuinely want and think I need, you know,"
[01:08:28]
"and I'm I want to be clear when I say this. I'm very happy. I'm very like pumped I got all the fucking that I got now. But it's like at the same time that that dissatisfaction, okay, when you're when you're not think about it like this, let me take a step back."
[01:08:45]
"A lot of people genuinely only perform when their backs against the wall. Okay, that's when a lot of people perform their best. It's when a lot of you in this very room have told me circumstantially you see your best performance. It's when you're in some fucking up situation that you got to sell yourself out of."
[01:09:05]
"Okay, you don't have to be back against the wall in order to constantly get richer. What do we have to do in a healthy way to perpetuate I want to get richer and make more money without having to be back against the wall?"
[01:09:20]
"We have to have the internal feeling and dialogue with oursel that constantly reiterates to us there's more I need to make. There's more I need to make. I have to get more. I have to do more for this. I have to get more for this."
[01:09:35]
"And that's not negative at all. That is probably the most positive internal dialogue you can have in the attempt to pursue getting richer and wealthier in your lifetime. Does that make sense?"
[01:09:50]
"It's the most positive thing that you could likely have internally going on because it is in itself what makes you want to get richer. Okay, that is the whole concept of you should be looking at that as positive, not as a negative. Okay,"
[01:10:08]
"poor people who are obsessed over money are obsessed over it because their backs against the wall. You're obsessed over money because you're trying to live better. You're trying to invest in more things."
[01:10:22]
"You're trying to perpetually over a longterm duration of time have so much accumulated resources and assets you get to do whatever the fuck you want, not give a fuck in the world about it."
[01:10:35]
"Okay, the point is freedom comes at the expense of that type of mental state right now while you're in your earning window. Eventually, when you've actually acquired enough, you can officially disengage with that kind of internal dialogue because you'll have achieved your financial goals."
[01:10:53]
"But for now, you're like fucking 20some years old. That should be your internal dialogue all fucking day and it should perpetuate the behavior to actually get richer as a conclusion of that internal dialogue happening all day. Does that make sense?"
[01:11:10]
"> Yeah."
[01:11:12]
"Good. Okay, other questions. All right, great. Give a round of applause. Go eat some food."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Investimento mencionado: $200K/mês (Jeremy), $42K/mês (Nick Louisiana), $0/mês (membro Europa)
Receitas comparadas: €300K/mês (Europa, net €200K), $100K/mês net (Louisiana)
Meta de riqueza: $100 milhões mínimo até 40s-60s para todos os membros
Projeção de investimento: $500K investido em 2024 = $4.38M em 23 anos (9.9% retorno, sem investir mais nada)
Projeção alternativa: $42K/mês por 15-20 anos = $60M+ mínimo
Retorno conservador: 9.9% anualizado (média S&P últimos 20 anos)
Exemplos de gastos "excess": $19K/mês aluguel casa, $25K/mês seguros de vida (4 apólices), $5K/mês quiroprático, $12K/ano hospital privado, $1K-$2K por refeição
Exemplos de bens: 12 carros (teve), 5 carros atuais, 3 PS5s, 40 camisetas idênticas, 12 pares sapatos idênticos, $400K em relógios, dezenas de milhares em armas
Transformações de membros: Cliente $2.5M/mês investiu em 40 empresas, Ryan $5M/mês, Leo $30K/mês (Corolla → Mercedes SUV)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Framework dos 4 Níveis Financeiros:
Survival: Perspectiva dia-a-dia/hora-a-hora, insegurança financeira constante
Comfort: $10K-$100K/mês, perspectiva mês-a-mês, primeiras experiências premium
Abundance: $100K-$200K+/mês, gastos sem pensar em itens antes importantes, staff sob demanda
Excess: Dinheiro além do necessário/desejável, início de investimentos massivos
Os 3 Transcendedores de Nível:
Income (Receita): Acumulação de dinheiro desbloqueia novos comportamentos automaticamente
People (Pessoas): Casamento, filhos, relacionamentos mudam identidade e comportamentos
Circumstances (Circunstâncias): Posses materiais, ambiente, situações atuam como âncoras ou elevadores
Estratégia de "Conditioning Up":
Fazer compras que causam desconforto no peito ($500 jantar, $300 camisa)
Sentir o desconforto, normalizar, repetir
Gradualmente elevar padrões até cruzar threshold para próximo nível
Proteção de Ambiente:
Eliminar pessoas/situações que puxam para baixo
Pagar para resolver problemas de proximidade (ex: Marina $5K para sair do emprego)
Rejeitar conversas/tópicos que não servem o nível desejado
Micro-Comportamentos de Excess:
Trocar escova de dente a cada 48h
Jogar fora pasta de dente no meio quando precisa apertar
Comprar múltiplas unidades do mesmo item (12 sapatos, 40 camisetas)
Nunca montar coisas compradas, nunca ir a supermercado
Serviços vêm até você (barber, dentist, doctor, X-ray em casa)
Não tolerar inconveniências (avião atrasado = cancelar viagem)
Estratégia de Ambiente Geográfico:
Louisiana (big fish) vs Miami (little fish)
Usar ambientes ricos para sentir-se pequeno e manter ambição
Manter ambiente local onde você é excess para fazer investimentos consistentes
ESTRUTURA:
Hierarquia clara: Survival → Comfort → Abundance → Excess (não pula níveis)
Mudanças inconscientes: Maioria das transições acontecem automaticamente baseado nos 3 transcendedores
Objetivo tornar consciente: Identificar exatamente o que é cada nível para VOCÊ pessoalmente
Âncoras vs Elevadores: Comportamentos atuais mantêm no nível atual, novos comportamentos elevam
Excess = Investimento: Só investe pesado quando não há mais uso necessário para o dinheiro
Obsessão positiva: Sentimento de "não é suficiente" é SAUDÁVEL e gera mais riqueza
Responsabilidade por outros: Identificar e elevar pessoas ao redor (Leo, Marina, staff)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Meta universal: $100M+ até 40s-60s para todos os membros
Método: Investimento consistente mensal + tempo + retorno conservador (9.9%)
Exemplo real Nick: $42K/mês → $500K em 2024 → $4.38M em 23 anos (sem mais investimentos)
Exemplo projetado: $42K/mês por 20 anos = $60M+ (com aumentos)
Fórmula: Não é provável chegar a $100M só com renda, precisa de investimentos compostos
Ação requerida: 1 ação por mês durante 20-30 anos (tão simples quanto ir à academia ou comer)
Realidade: Maioria está em "comfort" (precisa subir para abundance, depois excess para investir pesado)
DURAÇÃO: ~1h 11min
FORMATO: Palestra presencial em Miami para grupo de membros high-income, estilo coaching/masterclass com exemplos pessoais detalhados e case studies de membros do grupo
